refsect3.title: Byte Counts And Binary Data
refsect3.para:
Options, such as ipsec-max-bytes, that expect a numeric byte count, also accept decimal fractions, and the following multipliers as a suffix:
KiB
kilobytes (1024 bytes)
MiB
megabytes (1024*1024 bytes)
GiB
gigabytes (1024*1024*1024 bytes)
TiB
terabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)
PiB
petabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)
EiB
exabytes (1024*1024*1024*1024*1024*1024 bytes)

refsect3.title: Duration
refsect3.para:
Options, such as retransmit-timeout, that expect a numeric duration, also accept decimal fractions, and the following multipliers as a suffix:
us
microseconds (1/1_000_000 seconds)
ms
milliseconds (1/1_000 seconds)
s
seconds
m
minutes (60 seconds)
h
hours (60*60 seconds)
d
days (24*60*60 seconds)
w
weeks (7*24*60*60 seconds)
For instance, the following are each equivalent: 1ms and 1000us; 0.5s and 500ms; 1.5h and 90m.

refsect3.title: Proposals
refsect3.para: The options ike=, esp=, and ah= expect algorithm proposals to follow the following syntax:

Proposals are not required. When omitted, &Libreswan; will provide suitable defaults (see &ipsec-algparse.8;). Similarly, when an algorithm is omitted from a proposal, &Libreswan; will, again, provide suitable defaults.

Including KEM algorithms in either an &ESP; or &AH; proposal is not recommended. When a proposal requires KEM algorithms (for instance when PFS is enabled) the proposal will automatically include the KEM algorithm negotiated during the IKE exchange.
Enabled cryptographic algorithms and default proposals are logged by &pluto; during startup (see &ipsec-pluto.8;).

note.para: Some distributions, such as Fedora and RHEL/CentOS, use a System Wide Crypto Policy to specify the default ike= and esp= proposals. Specifying your own ike= (or esp=) proposal means overriding all these system or software recommended defaults, but can be necessary at times.
Using a PRF (KDF) algorithm that is different to the INTEGRITY (hash) algorithm is not recommended.

refsect3.para: The ";TYPE=ALGORITHMS" syntax was added in &Libreswan; 5.4. Support for "ENCRYPTION-INTEGRITY-PRF" in &IKE; proposals was deprecated in &Libreswan; 5.4. Support for "ENCRYPTION-PRF-INTEGRITY" in &IKE; proposals was added in &Libreswan; 5.4, however, other than to work-around interoperability problems its, use is strongly discouraged.
